---
title: "TMLE in the Clean-Room Framework for Pharmacoepidemiology"
subtitle: "Pre-specified analysis with locked code, reproducible pipelines, and audit trails"
format: html
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## In development
This chapter is under active development. The content below is a placeholder. A full chapter on clean-room TMLE workflows for regulatory pharmacoepidemiology is planned.

This chapter will be built around the [`cleanTMLE`](https://github.com/amertens/cleanTMLE) R package, currently under development, which provides a pre-specified TMLE pipeline for clean-room analysis. The package implements locked analysis plans, automated diagnostics, and reproducible audit trails suitable for regulatory submissions.

Follow development at: <https://github.com/amertens/cleanTMLE>
:::

*Estimated reading time: TBD*

# Planned content

This chapter will cover:

- Why pre-specification matters for regulatory RWE
- The clean-room constraint: no iteration after data access
- How TMLE with Super Learner fits the clean-room workflow
- The `cleanTMLE` package: interface, pre-specification, and audit logging
- A worked example using the package
- Regulatory auditability: what must be documented
- Comparison with traditional regression-based pre-specified analyses
